beats.narration_text: Habari. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of tunneling wave packet. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of tunneling wave packet

beats.narration_text: Tunneling Wave Packet is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Crank-Nicolson TDSE: a Gaussian splits at a rectangular barrier — reflected and transmitted components emerge simultaneously.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Tunneling Wave Packet. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Barrier height V₀ (eV), Barrier width L (nm), Electron energy E (eV). Verify: Crank-Nicolson TDSE: a Gaussian splits at a rectangular barrier — reflected and transmitted components emerge simultaneo. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Tunneling Wave Packet. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: Barrier height V₀ (eV), Barrier width L (nm), Ele

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from 11-tunneling-wave-packet.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: tunneling wave packet. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag Barrier height V₀ (eV) from 1.2 to 4.0. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of Barrier height V₀ (eV) from 1.2 to 4.0

beats.narration_text: Crank-Nicolson TDSE: a Gaussian splits at a rectangular barrier — reflected and transmitted components emerge simultaneously.. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of tunneling wave packet. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Tunneling Wave Packet simulation and add a second slider that controls Barrier height V₀ (eV). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Tunneling Wave Packet simulation and add a second slider that controls Barrier height V₀ (eV). Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CD
